rationale: Iran deal momentum sustains mild energy price relief hopes for Portugal; no other events with direct Portuguese impact this cycle.

top_events.title: Iran: deal would lead to Hormuz reopening
top_events.summary: Continued Iran-US diplomatic momentum keeps alive the prospect of lower global energy prices, which would benefit Portugal's import-dependent economy and cost of living.

top_events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from climate-hit countries
top_events.summary: US immigration crackdown on nationals from climate-vulnerable countries could affect some Portuguese-speaking communities (e.g. Cape Verde, Brazil diaspora links), but impact on Portugal itself is minimal.

top_events.title: World Cup economics and tourism buzz
top_events.summary: FIFA World Cup in the US generates tourism and economic activity; Portugal's team participation could boost national morale and some economic activity, but no direct cost-of-living impact.
